keyPoints: Ethical principles (e.g., fairness, transparency) must be translated into concrete technical requirements and design specifications.
Governance frameworks, including audits, impact assessments, and oversight boards, are necessary to operationalize ethics.
A multi-stakeholder approach involving ethicists, developers, policymakers, and the public is crucial for effective implementation.
commonMistakes: Assuming a set of high-level principles is sufficient without creating specific, actionable processes.
Treating ethics as a one-time checklist rather than an ongoing, iterative practice integrated throughout the development lifecycle.
Focusing only on technical implementation while ignoring the necessary organizational culture and governance structures.
